comment.text: This key displays a message explaining the access granted to a user, specifying their access type, rights, and the content/parent they can access. It's used to clarify sharing permissions within the UI, likely in a sharing dialog or permissions management section. Translators should ensure the phrasing accurately reflects the level of access being described.

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Azerbaijani translation changes the grammatical structure in a way that makes the sentence awkward and potentially changes the relationship between the variables. The English structure is 'X will be able to [do Y] to [Z]', whereas the Azerbaijani version translates to 'X will have the ability to [do Y] the [Z] part of [Parent]', which is a semantic shift in how permissions are applied.

The German translation changes the meaning from 'will be able to [access rights]' to 'can [access rights] share'. The English sentence describes the capability/permission granted, whereas the German version incorrectly implies the action of sharing the content itself.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: <1>{{accessType}}</1> wird in der Lage sein, {{shareContents}} von {{shareParent}} zu <3>{{accessRights}}</3>.

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The variable {{shareContents}} is missing from the Armenian translation.

The Polish translation 'zawartości {{shareContents}} z {{shareParent}}' is grammatically incorrect and changes the meaning. In the English source, '{{shareContents}}' is the object being acted upon, and '{{shareParent}}' is the container. The Polish version translates to 'contents {{shareContents}} with {{shareParent}}', which is nonsensical in this context.
Extra variable(s) not in English: {{ accessType}}
The variable '{{accessType}}' has an extra space inside the braces ('{{ accessType}}'), which breaks the variable matching in the code.

The Romanian translation changes the meaning from 'will be able to [action]' to 'are permissions of [action]', which is grammatically incorrect and fails to convey the intended action/capability of the access type.

The Sinhala translation reorders the logic in a way that makes the sentence grammatically awkward or potentially incorrect depending on the variable values. Specifically, '{{shareParent}} හි' (of {{shareParent}}) is placed before the rights/contents, whereas the English structure links the action directly to the parent at the end. More importantly, the Sinhala structure '{{accessType}} හට ... කිරීමට හැකි වනු ඇත' ({{accessType}} will be able to ...) is a valid construction, but the placement of the variables makes the relationship between 'accessRights' and 'shareContents' ambiguous compared to the English source.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: <1>{{accessType}}</1> හට {{shareParent}} හි {{shareContents}} සඳහා <3>{{accessRights}}</3> කිරීමට හැකි වනු ඇත.

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Turkish translation changes the meaning from 'will be able to [access rights]' to 'will be able to share [access rights]'. The English text describes what the user can DO with the content, whereas the Turkish text describes the act of sharing the content itself.
The variable {{shareContents}} is missing from the Chinese translation.
